1→#!/usr/bin/env python3
     2→"""Circuit optimization and analysis tool."""

     4→import sys

     6→from dataclasses import dataclass
     7→from typing import Optional, List, Dict, Any, Tuple, Set

    13→# =============================================================================
    14→# Error Classes
    15→# =============================================================================

    17→class CircoptError(Exception):
    18→    """Base exception for circopt errors."""
    19→    pass
    20→
    21→
    22→class CliUsageError(CircoptError):
    23→    """CLI usage error - exit code 1."""
    24→    pass
    25→
    26→
    27→class FileNotFoundError(CircoptError):
    28→    """File not found - exit code 1."""
    29→    pass
    30→
    31→
    32→class CircParseError(CircoptError):
    33→    """Parse error in circuit file - exit code 2."""
    34→    def __init__(self, message: str, file: str, line: int, col: int):
    35→        super().__init__(message)
    36→        self.file = file
    37→        self.line = line
    38→        self.col = col
    39→
    40→
    41→class DeclarationAfterAssignmentError(CircoptError):
    42→    """Declaration after assignment - exit code 3."""
    43→    pass
    44→
    45→
    46→class DuplicateNameError(CircoptError):
    47→    """Duplicate name declaration - exit code 3."""
    48→    pass
    49→
    50→
    51→class UndefinedNameError(CircoptError):
    52→    """Undefined name used - exit code 3."""
    53→    pass
    54→
    55→
    56→class UnassignedSignalError(CircoptError):
    57→    """Signal not assigned - exit code 3."""
    58→    pass
    59→
    60→
    61→class InputAssignmentError(CircoptError):
    62→    """Tried to assign to input - exit code 3."""
    63→    pass
    64→
    65→
    66→class MultipleAssignmentError(CircoptError):
    67→    """Multiple assignments to same signal - exit code 3."""
    68→    pass
    69→
    70→
    71→class ArityError(CircoptError):
    72→    """Wrong operator arity - exit code 3."""
    73→    pass

    75→
    76→class CycleError(CircoptError):
    77→    """Cycle detected in assignment graph - exit code 3."""
    78→    def __init__(self, message: str, cycle: List[str]):
    79→        super().__init__(message)
    80→        self.cycle = cycle
    81→
    82→
    83→# =============================================================================
    84→# AST Nodes
    85→# =============================================================================
    86→
    87→@dataclass
    88→class Signal:
    89→    name: str
    90→    is_input: bool = False
    91→    is_output: bool = False
    92→    assigned: bool = False
    93→
    94→
    95→@dataclass
    96→class Expr:
    97→    pass
    98→
    99→
   100→@dataclass
   101→class ExprIdentifier(Expr):
   102→    name: str
   103→
   104→
   105→@dataclass
   106→class ExprLiteral(Expr):
   107→    value: bool  # True for 1, False for 0
   108→
   109→
   110→@dataclass
   111→class ExprCall(Expr):
   112→    op: str
   113→    args: List[Expr]
   114→
   115→
   116→@dataclass
   117→class Assignment:
   118→    lhs: str
   119→    expr: Expr
   120→    line: int
   121→    col: int
   122→
   123→
   124→# =============================================================================
   125→# Parser
   126→# =============================================================================
   127→
   128→# Operator arities
   129→OPERATOR_ARITIES = {
   130→    'NOT': 1,
   131→    'BUF': 1,
   132→    'AND': 2,   # minimum
   133→    'OR': 2,
   134→    'XOR': 2,
   135→    'NAND': 2,
   136→    'NOR': 2,
   137→    'XNOR': 2,
   138→}
   139→
   140→VALID_OPERATORS = set(OPERATOR_ARITIES.keys())
   141→
   142→
   143→class Parser:
   144→    def __init__(self, text: str, filename: str):
   145→        self.text = text
   146→        self.filename = filename
   147→        self.lines = text.split('\n')
   148→        self.line_num = 0
   149→        self.col_num = 0
   150→        self.pos = 0
   151→        self.len = len(text)
   152→
   153→        # Track declarations phase
   154→        self.in_declarations = True
   155→        self.inputs: Set[str] = set()
   156→        self.outputs: Set[str] = set()
   157→        self.wires: Set[str] = set()
   158→        self.all_names: Set[str] = set()
   159→
   160→        # Track assignments
   161→        self.assignments: List[Assignment] = []
   162→        self.assigned_names: Set[str] = set()
   163→
   164→    def peek(self) -> Optional[str]:
   165→        """Peek at next character without consuming."""
   166→        if self.pos >= self.len:
   167→            return None
   168→        return self.text[self.pos]
   169→
   170→    def get_char(self) -> Optional[str]:
   171→        """Get next character and advance."""
   172→        if self.pos >= self.len:
   173→            return None
   174→        char = self.text[self.pos]
   175→        self.pos += 1
   176→        if char == '\n':
   177→            self.line_num += 1
   178→            self.col_num = 0
   179→        else:
   180→            self.col_num += 1
   181→        return char
   182→
   183→    def skip_whitespace(self):
   184→        """Skip whitespace and comments."""
   185→        while self.pos < self.len:
   186→            char = self.peek()
   187→            if char is None:
   188→                break
   189→            if char.isspace():
   190→                self.get_char()
   191→            elif char == '#':
   192→                # Skip comment to end of line
   193→                while self.pos < self.len:
   194→                    c = self.get_char()
   195→                    if c == '\n' or c is None:
   196→                        break
   197→            else:
   198→                break
   199→
   200→    def get_identifier(self) -> str:
   201→        """Parse an identifier."""
   202→        char = self.peek()
   203→        if not char or (not char.isalpha() and char != '_'):
   204→            raise self.error("Expected identifier")
   205→
   206→        result = []
   207→        while self.pos < self.len:
   208→            c = self.peek()
   209→            if c is None:
   210→                break
   211→            if c.isalnum() or c == '_':
   212→                result.append(self.get_char())
   213→            else:
   214→                break
   215→
   216→        return ''.join(result)
   217→
   218→    def get_name_list(self) -> List[str]:
   219→        """Parse a space-separated list of names."""
   220→        names = []
   221→        while True:
   222→            self.skip_whitespace()
   223→            if self.peek() is None:
   224→                break
   225→            name = self.get_identifier().upper()  # Normalize for keywords
   226→            if not name:
   227→                break
   228→            names.append(name)
   229→            self.skip_whitespace()
   230→
   231→        return names
   232→
   233→    def parse_expr(self) -> Expr:
   234→        """Parse an expression."""
   235→        self.skip_whitespace()
   236→
   237→        # Check for identifier
   238→        if self.peek() and (self.peek().isalpha() or self.peek() == '_'):
   239→            ident = self.get_identifier()
   240→            # Check if it's a function call
   241→            self.skip_whitespace()
   242→            if self.peek() == '(':
   243→                self.get_char()  # consume '('
   244→                args = self.parse_expr_list()
   245→                self.skip_whitespace()
   246→                if self.peek() != ')':
   247→                    raise self.error("Expected ')'")
   248→                self.get_char()  # consume ')'
   249→
   250→                op = ident.upper()
   251→                if op not in VALID_OPERATORS:
   252→                    raise self.error(f"Unknown operator '{ident}'")
   253→                return ExprCall(op, args)
   254→            else:
   255→                return ExprIdentifier(ident)
   256→
   257→        # Check for literal
   258→        if self.peek() and self.peek().isdigit():
   259→            digit = self.get_char()
   260→            if digit == '0':
   261→                return ExprLiteral(False)
   262→            elif digit == '1':
   263→                return ExprLiteral(True)
   264→            elif digit == 'X':
   265→                raise self.error("Literal 'X' is not allowed")
   266→
   267→        raise self.error("Expected expression")
   268→
   269→    def parse_expr_list(self) -> List[Expr]:
   270→        """Parse a comma-separated list of expressions."""
   271→        exprs = []
   272→        self.skip_whitespace()
   273→
   274→        if self.peek() == ')':
   275→            return exprs
   276→
   277→        while True:
   278→            exprs.append(self.parse_expr())
   279→            self.skip_whitespace()
   280→            if self.peek() == ',':
   281→                self.get_char()
   282→                self.skip_whitespace()
   283→                continue
   284→            break
   285→
   286→        return exprs
   287→
   288→    def parse_line(self) -> Optional[Tuple[str, List[str]]]:
   289→        """Parse a single line. Returns (type, data) or None."""
   290→        self.skip_whitespace()
   291→        if self.peek() is None:
   292→            return None
   293→
   294→        # Check for comment or empty line
   295→        if self.peek() == '#':
   296→            # Skip to end of line
   297→            while self.pos < self.len:
   298→                c = self.get_char()
   299→                if c == '\n' or c is None:
   300→                    break
   301→            return None
   302→
   303→        start_pos = self.pos
   304→        start_line = self.line_num
   305→        start_col = self.col_num
   306→
   307→        # Check for declaration
   308→        if self.peek() and (self.peek().isalpha() or self.peek() == '_'):
   309→            ident = self.get_identifier()
   310→            self.skip_whitespace()
   311→
   312→            # Declaration line
   313→            if ident.lower() in ('input', 'output', 'wire'):
   314→                names = self.get_name_list()
   315→                if ident.lower() == 'wire' and len(names) == 0:
   316→                    pass  # Empty wire declaration is allowed
   317→                return (ident.lower(), names, start_line, start_col)
   318→
   319→            # Assignment line
   320→            if self.peek() == '=':
   321→                self.get_char()  # consume '='
   322→                expr = self.parse_expr()
   323→                self.skip_whitespace()
   324→                if self.peek() is not None:
   325→                    raise self.error(f"Unexpected character after expression")
   326→
   327→                lhs = ident
   328→                return ('assignment', lhs, expr, start_line, start_col)
   329→
   330→            raise self.error(f"Unexpected '{ident}'")
   331→
   332→        raise self.error(f"Unexpected character '{self.peek()}'")
   333→
   334→    def error(self, message: str) -> CircParseError:
   335→        """Create a parse error at current position."""
   336→        return CircParseError(
   337→            message,
   338→            self.filename,
   339→            self.line_num + 1,  # 1-based
   340→            self.col_num + 1 if self.col_num > 0 else 1
   341→        )
   342→
   343→    def parse(self):
   344→        """Parse the entire file."""
   345→        # First pass: collect declarations and assignments
   346→        while self.pos < self.len:
   347→            result = self.parse_line()
   348→            if result is None:
   349→                continue
   350→
   351→            line_type = result[0]
   352→
   353→            if line_type in ('input', 'output', 'wire'):
   354→                decl_type, names, line, col = result
   355→
   356→                # Check if we've already seen assignments
   357→                if self.in_declarations and len(self.assignments) > 0:
   358→                    raise DeclarationAfterAssignmentError(
   359→                        f"Declaration after assignment at line {line}"
   360→                    )
   361→
   362→                for name in names:
   363→                    # Normalize: preserve case for identifiers, but check for duplicates
   364→                    name_lower = name.lower()
   365→
   366→                    # Check for duplicates
   367→                    if name in self.all_names:
   368→                        raise DuplicateNameError(
   369→                            f"Duplicate name '{name}'"
   370→                        )
   371→
   372→                    self.all_names.add(name)
   373→
   374→                    if decl_type == 'input':
   375→                        self.inputs.add(name)
   376→                    elif decl_type == 'output':
   377→                        self.outputs.add(name)
   378→                    else:  # wire
   379→                        self.wires.add(name)
   380→
   381→            elif line_type == 'assignment':
   382→                _, lhs, expr, line, col = result
   383→                self.in_declarations = False
   384→                self.assignments.append(Assignment(lhs, expr, line, col))
   385→
   386→            # Move to next line
   387→            while self.pos < self.len:
   388→                c = self.get_char()
   389→                if c == '\n' or c is None:
   390→                    break
   391→
   392→        return {
   393→            'inputs': self.inputs,
   394→            'outputs': self.outputs,
   395→            'wires': self.wires,
   396→            'assignments': self.assignments
   397→        }
   398→
   399→
   400→# =============================================================================
   401→# Validator
   402→# =============================================================================
   403→
   404→class Validator:
   405→    def __init__(self, parse_result: dict):
   406→        self.inputs = parse_result['inputs']
   407→        self.outputs = parse_result['outputs']
   408→        self.wires = parse_result['wires']
   409→        self.assignments = parse_result['assignments']
   410→
   411→        self.all_signals = self.inputs | self.outputs | self.wires
   412→        self.assigned_from: Dict[str, List[str]] = defaultdict(list)  # signal -> list of signals it depends on
   413→        self.signal_users: Dict[str, List[str]] = defaultdict(list)   # signal -> list of signals that use it
   414→
   415→    def validate(self):
   416→        """Run all validation checks."""
   417→        # Check that all LHS are valid
   418→        for assign in self.assignments:
   419→            self._check_lhs(assign)
   420→
   421→        # Check that all RHS identifiers are defined
   422→        for assign in self.assignments:
   423→            self._check_rhs_defined(assign.expr)
   424→
   425→        # Check operator arities
   426→        for assign in self.assignments:
   427→            self._check_arity(assign.expr)
   428→
   429→        # Check all signals are assigned exactly once
   430→        self._check_assignment_completeness()
   431→
   432→        # Check for cycles
   433→        self._check_cycles()
   434→
   435→    def _check_lhs(self, assign: Assignment):
   436→        """Check LHS is valid."""
   437→        lhs = assign.lhs
   438→
   439→        if lhs in self.inputs:
   440→            raise InputAssignmentError(f"Cannot assign to input '{lhs}'")
   441→
   442→        if lhs not in self.outputs and lhs not in self.wires:
   443→            raise UndefinedNameError(f"Undefined signal '{lhs}'")
   444→
   445→    def _check_rhs_defined(self, expr: Expr):
   446→        """Check all identifiers in expression are defined."""
   447→        if isinstance(expr, ExprIdentifier):
   448→            if expr.name not in self.all_signals:
   449→                raise UndefinedNameError(f"Undefined signal '{expr.name}'")
   450→        elif isinstance(expr, ExprCall):
   451→            for arg in expr.args:
   452→                self._check_rhs_defined(arg)
   453→
   454→    def _check_arity(self, expr: Expr):
   455→        """Check operator arity."""
   456→        if isinstance(expr, ExprCall):
   457→            op = expr.op
   458→            min_arity = OPERATOR_ARITIES[op]
   459→            actual_arity = len(expr.args)
   460→
   461→            if actual_arity < min_arity:
   462→                raise ArityError(
   463→                    f"Operator '{op}' requires at least {min_arity} arguments, got {actual_arity}"
   464→                )
   465→
   466→            for arg in expr.args:
   467→                self._check_arity(arg)
   468→
   469→    def _check_assignment_completeness(self):
   470→        """Check all wires and outputs are assigned exactly once."""
   471→        assigned = set()
   472→
   473→        for assign in self.assignments:
   474→            lhs = assign.lhs
   475→            if lhs in assigned:
   476→                raise MultipleAssignmentError(
   477→                    f"Multiple assignments to '{lhs}'"
   478→                )
   479→            assigned.add(lhs)
   480→
   481→        # Check for unassigned signals
   482→        for wire in self.wires:
   483→            if wire not in assigned:
   484→                raise UnassignedSignalError(f"Unassigned wire '{wire}'")
   485→
   486→        for output in self.outputs:
   487→            if output not in assigned:
   488→                raise UnassignedSignalError(f"Unassigned output '{output}'")
   489→
   490→    def _check_cycles(self):
   491→        """Check for cycles in the assignment graph."""
   492→        # Build dependency graph
   493→        for assign in self.assignments:
   494→            lhs = assign.lhs
   495→            deps = self._collect_deps(assign.expr)
   496→            self.assigned_from[lhs] = deps
   497→            for dep in deps:
   498→                self.signal_users[dep].append(lhs)
   499→
   500→        # Check for cycles using DFS
   501→        visited = set()
   502→        rec_stack = set()
   503→        path = []
   504→
   505→        def dfs(node: str) -> Optional[List[str]]:
   506→            visited.add(node)
   507→            rec_stack.add(node)
   508→            path.append(node)
   509→
   510→            for neighbor in self.assigned_from.get(node, []):
   511→                if neighbor not in visited:
   512→                    cycle = dfs(neighbor)
   513→                    if cycle:
   514→                        return cycle
   515→                elif neighbor in rec_stack:
   516→                    # Found a cycle
   517→                    cycle_start = path.index(neighbor)
   518→                    return path[cycle_start:] + [neighbor]
   519→
   520→            path.pop()
   521→            rec_stack.remove(node)
   522→            return None
   523→
   524→        # Start from each unvisited signal
   525→        for signal in self.all_signals:
   526→            if signal not in visited:
   527→                cycle = dfs(signal)
   528→                if cycle:
   529→                    cycle_path = ' -> '.join(cycle)
   530→                    raise CycleError(
   531→                        f"Cycle detected: {cycle_path}",
   532→                        cycle
   533→                    )
   534→
   535→    def _collect_deps(self, expr: Expr) -> List[str]:
   536→        """Collect all signal dependencies from an expression."""
   537→        deps = []
   538→        if isinstance(expr, ExprIdentifier):
   539→            deps.append(expr.name)
   540→        elif isinstance(expr, ExprCall):
   541→            for arg in expr.args:
   542→                deps.extend(self._collect_deps(arg))
   543→        return deps

   546→# =============================================================================
   547→# Circuit Interface
   548→# =============================================================================

   551→class Interface:
   552→    inputs: List[Dict[str, Any]]
   553→    outputs: List[Dict[str, Any]]

   556→def get_circuit_interface(inputs: Set[str], outputs: Set[str]) -> Interface:
   557→    """Get the circuit interface (inputs and outputs sorted lexicographically)."""
   558→    input_list = sorted([{'name': name, 'msb': 0, 'lsb': 0} for name in inputs],
   559→                        key=lambda x: x['name'])
   560→    output_list = sorted([{'name': name, 'msb': 0, 'lsb': 0} for name in outputs],
   561→                         key=lambda x: x['name'])

   572→    result.update(kwargs)
   573→    print(json.dumps(result))
   574→
   575→
   576→def print_json_error(command: str, exit_code: int, error_type: str,
   577→                     message: str, file: Optional[str] = None,
   578→                     line: Optional[int] = None, col: Optional[int] = None):
   579→    """Print JSON error response."""

   581→    if file is not None:
   582→        error_obj['file'] = file
   583→    if line is not None:
   584→        error_obj['line'] = line
   585→    if col is not None:
   586→        error_obj['col'] = col
   587→
   588→    result = {
   589→        'ok': False,
   590→        'command': command,
   591→        'exit_code': exit_code,
   592→        'error': error_obj
   593→    }
   594→    print(json.dumps(result))
   595→
   596→
   597→def get_exit_code(error: Exception) -> int:
   598→    """Get the exit code for an error."""
   599→    if isinstance(error, (CliUsageError, FileNotFoundError)):
   600→        return 1
   601→    elif isinstance(error, CircParseError):
   602→        return 2
   603→    elif isinstance(error, (DeclarationAfterAssignmentError, DuplicateNameError,
   604→                            UndefinedNameError, UnassignedSignalError,
   605→                            InputAssignmentError, MultipleAssignmentError,
   606→                            ArityError, CycleError)):
   607→        return 3
   608→    elif isinstance(error, CircoptError):
   609→        return 4
   610→    else:
   611→        return 4
   612→
   613→
   614→def get_error_type(error: Exception) -> str:
   615→    """Get the error type name."""
   616→    return type(error).__name__

   618→
   619→def check_file(filename: str, use_json: bool) -> int:
   620→    """Check a .circ file. Returns exit code."""
   621→    try:
   622→        # Read file
   623→        try:
   624→            with open(filename, 'r') as f:
   625→                text = f.read()
   626→        except FileNotFoundError:
   627→            if use_json:
   628→                print_json_error(
   629→                    'check',
   630→                    1, 'FileNotFoundError',
   631→                    f"File not found: {filename}"
   632→                )
   633→            else:
   634→                print(f"Error: File not found: {filename}")
   635→            return 1
   636→
   637→        # Parse
   638→        parser = Parser(text, filename)
   639→        parse_result = parser.parse()
   640→
   641→        # Validate
   642→        validator = Validator(parse_result)
   643→        validator.validate()
   644→
   645→        # Success
   646→        if use_json:
   647→            interface = get_circuit_interface(parse_result['inputs'], parse_result['outputs'])
   648→            print_json_ok('check', format='circ',
   649→                         inputs=interface.inputs,
   650→                         outputs=interface.outputs)
   651→        else:
   652→            interface = get_circuit_interface(parse_result['inputs'], parse_result['outputs'])
   653→            print(f"inputs: {[i['name'] for i in interface.inputs]}")
   654→            print(f"outputs: {[o['name'] for o in interface.outputs]}")
   655→
   656→        return 0
   657→
   658→    except CircParseError as e:
   659→        if use_json:
   660→            print_json_error('check', 2, 'CircParseError', str(e),
   661→                           file=e.file, line=e.line, col=e.col)
   662→        else:
   663→            print(f"Parse error at {e.file}:{e.line}:{e.col}: {e}")
   664→        return 2
   665→
   666→    except (DeclarationAfterAssignmentError, DuplicateNameError,
   667→            UndefinedNameError, UnassignedSignalError, InputAssignmentError,
   668→            MultipleAssignmentError, ArityError, CycleError) as e:
   669→
   670→        error_type = get_error_type(e)
   671→
   672→        if isinstance(e, CycleError):
   673→            message = str(e)
   674→        else:
   675→            message = str(e)
   676→
   677→        if use_json:
   678→            print_json_error('check', 3, error_type, message)
   679→        else:
   680→            print(f"Validation error: {message}")
   681→        return 3
   682→
   683→    except Exception as e:
   684→        if use_json:
   685→            print_json_error('check', 4, get_error_type(e), str(e))
   686→        else:
   687→            print(f"Internal error: {e}")
   688→        return 4

   691→def print_help():
   692→    """Print help text."""
   693→    help_text = """Usage: circopt.py [OPTIONS] COMMAND [ARGS]...
   694→
   695→Options:
   696→  --help          Show this help message and exit
   697→  --version       Show version information and exit
   698→  --json          Output JSON instead of plain text
   699→
   700→Commands:
   701→  check           Check a .circ file for validity
   702→"""
   703→    print(help_text)
   704→
   705→
   706→def print_version(json_mode: bool):
   707→    """Print version."""
   708→    if json_mode:
   709→        print_json_ok('__version__', version=VERSION)
   710→    else:
   711→        print(VERSION)
   712→
   713→
   714→def main(argv: List[str]) -> int:
   715→    """Main entry point."""
   716→    if not argv:
   717→        print_json_error('__cli__', 1, 'CliUsageError',
   718→                        "No command provided")
   719→        return 1
   720→
   721→    # Parse global flags
   722→    args = argv[:]
   723→    json_mode = False
   724→    command = None
   725→    command_args = []
   726→
   727→    # Extract --json flag if present (before command)
   728→    i = 0
   729→    while i < len(args):
   730→        if args[i] == '--json':
   731→            json_mode = True
   732→            args.pop(i)
   733→        else:
   734→            i += 1
   735→
   736→    # Check for help/version before command
   737→    if '--help' in args:
   738→        print_help()

   740→
   741→    if '--version' in args:
   742→        print_version(json_mode)

   744→
   745→    # Get command
   746→    if not args:
   747→        if json_mode:
   748→            print_json_error('__cli__', 1, 'CliUsageError',
   749→                           "No command provided")
   750→        else:

   753→
   754→    command = args[0]

   767→        filename = command_args[0]

   770→    else:

   774→        else:

            json_mode = True
            args.pop(i)
        else:
            i += 1

    # Check for help/version before command
    if '--help' in args:
        print_help()
